title: MemoryCell
description: A single unit of agent memory in the Agent Memory Protocol (AMP) v0.1.0.

amp_version.description: AMP spec version this cell conforms to.

id.description: Globally unique ULID for this cell, prefixed with 'mem_'.

content.description: The payload of the memory cell.

text.description: The memory content in natural language.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs for indexing or filtering.

identity.description: Ownership and provenance identity for the cell.

owner_id.description: ID of the user, agent, or organization that owns this memory.

created_by.description: ID of the agent or system that created this cell. Defaults to the X-AMP-Agent-ID request header value.

session_id.description: Optional session context in which this memory was created.

lifecycle.description: Temporal state and status of the memory cell.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the cell was created. Set by the server; not writable by clients.

last_accessed_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent GET /memories/{id} call. Updated by the server.

last_updated_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent PATCH. Updated by the server.

expires_at.description: Optional hard expiry timestamp. The server MAY delete the cell after this time.

status.description: Current lifecycle status. Transitions are managed by the lifecycle engine.

scoring.description: Relevance and decay scoring for the lifecycle engine.

importance.description: Subjective importance of this memory, used in decay calculation.

confidence.description: Confidence in the accuracy of the memory content.

decay_rate.description: Daily exponential decay rate applied to the combined score (importance × confidence × e^(−rate × days)).

access_count.description: Number of times this cell has been retrieved. Incremented by the server on each GET.

access_policy.description: Read and write access control lists. Owner and creator always retain full access.

readable_by.description: List of agent ID patterns (supports fnmatch wildcards, e.g. 'agent-team-*') allowed to read this cell.

writable_by.description: List of agent ID patterns allowed to update or delete this cell.

public.description: If true, any authenticated agent may read this cell regardless of readable_by.

provenance.description: Origin metadata describing how and where this memory was created.

source_type.description: Category of the source that produced this memory.

source_ref.description: Opaque reference to the source artifact (e.g. a conversation ID or document URI).

extraction_method.description: Method used to extract this memory from the source.

content.text: User prefers Python for backend development
